description:
Spec: docs/cps-in-clif.md §8.2 — top-level fns wrapped at module init into static zero-capture closures.

Work:
- At module-compile time, for every fn `f` used anywhere as a `Prim::MakeClosure(f, [])` target (zero-capture only at this ticket; non-empty captures stay dynamic), allocate one closure singleton at module-init time and expose its address as a Cranelift global value `@static_closure_<fid>`.
- Allocation path: extend `runtime/src/ir_runtime.rs` with `fz_alloc_static_closure(fid: u32, code_ptr: u64) -> *mut u8` or reuse `fz_alloc_closure(fid, 0)` from a module-init hook.
- Codegen: replace `Prim::MakeClosure(fid, [])` lowering with a global_value load of the singleton; non-zero capture path unchanged.
- Module init shape: a per-module `init_static_closures(process)` runtime hook called by `make_process`/scheduler-spawn before first dispatch; populates a `Vec<*mut u8>` keyed by fid → singleton address. Cranelift codegen emits `iconst.i64 <ptr>` for the singleton at use sites (resolved at JIT time, not via global_value, since process-local heap addresses aren't known until init).
  - Alternative: emit a `call %fz_get_static_closure(fid)` at each use site that looks up the per-process singleton. Slower but avoids ptr-baking.
  - Pick whichever is cleaner; document in ticket comments.

Acceptance:
- New unit test: `static_zero_capture_closure_singleton_reused` — same MakeClosure target accessed twice produces identical pointer.
- `higher_order.fz` clif disassembly: `double`/`neg` MakeClosure sites lower to a single load/lookup, not `fz_alloc_closure(_, 0)`.
- §8.2 module-init region produces exactly one static closure per zero-capture target.
title: fz-cps.1.7: static zero-capture closure singletons
